javax.media.mscontrol.spi
Class DriverManager

java.lang.Object
  javax.media.mscontrol.spi.DriverManager

public class DriverManager
extends java.lang.Object

Service for managing a set of Media Server Control API drivers.

As part of its initialization, the DriverManager, will load all drivers packages as jar files including a file META-INF/services/javax.media.mscontrol.spi.Driver. This file contains the name of the driver's implementation of javax.media.mscontrol.spi.Driver.

Drivers packaged this way, does not have to explicitly register in the DriverManager. Drivers packaged differently must explictly register in the DriverManager using the registerDriver method.

When the method getFactory is called, the DriverManager will look up the requested driver and retrieve a MsControlFactory from it.

See Also:
Driver, MsControlFactory

Method Summary
static void deregisterDriver(Driver driver)
          Removes the given Driver from the DriverManager's list of available Drivers.
static Driver getDriver(java.lang.String driverName)
          Retrieves the driver with the given driver name.
static java.util.Iterator<Driver> getDrivers()
          Retrieves an Iterator of all drivers currently registered in the DriverManager.
static MsControlFactory getFactory(java.lang.String driverName, java.util.Properties info)
          Retrieves a MsControlFactory instance from the specified driver.
static void registerDriver(Driver driver)
          Registers the given driver with the DriverManager.
 
Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object
equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait
 

Method Detail

registerDriver

public static void registerDriver(Driver driver)
Registers the given driver with the DriverManager. A driver not packaged as a service provider' , must call the method registerDriver to make itself know to the DriverManager.

Parameters:
driver - the Driver to be registered with the DriverManager

deregisterDriver

public static void deregisterDriver(Driver driver)
Removes the given Driver from the DriverManager's list of available Drivers.

Parameters:
driver - the Driver to be removed from the DriverManager's list of Drivers

getFactory

public static MsControlFactory getFactory(java.lang.String driverName,
                                          java.util.Properties info)
                                   throws MsControlException
Retrieves a MsControlFactory instance from the specified driver. The returned MsControlFactory instance must be configured according to the info argument.

Parameters:
driverName - name of the driver to provide the factory
info - properties containing implementation specific key-value pairs, see also MEDIA_SERVER_URI
Returns:
returns a factory instance from the specified driver
Throws:
MsControlException - If the specified driver is not registered with the DriverManager, or if the driver is not able to provide a factory due to invalid/missing properties passed in the info argument

getDrivers

public static java.util.Iterator<Driver> getDrivers()
Retrieves an Iterator of all drivers currently registered in the DriverManager. Note that invoking remove() on the iterator does not de-register the current Driver in the DriverManager.

Returns:
all registered drivers
Throws:
java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - If remove() is invoked on the returned Iterator

getDriver

public static Driver getDriver(java.lang.String driverName)
Retrieves the driver with the given driver name.

Parameters:
driverName - name of the driver
Returns:
The driver with the given driver name. null in case no such driver is registered in the DriverManager.
